mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack indicates severe aggregate demand deficiency and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or positive returns occur in <45% of co-occurring up months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side momentum and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive INDPRO change months, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the correlation between GDP growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.10 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, rising inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95.0 during late-cycle expansions, capacity constraints and inflation fears dominate, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of observations above the threshold, or the signal fails to differentiate returns between early and late cycle regimes.
